examples.title: Default Assignment Operator
examples.description: Demonstrates default shallow copy assignment operator
examples.explanation: Default assignment performs member-wise copy. For pointers, it copies the address, not the data, leading to potential issues.

examples.title: Deep Copy Assignment Operator
examples.description: Proper deep copy assignment operator implementation
examples.explanation: Deep copy assignment allocates new memory and copies actual data, creating independent objects.

examples.title: Copy Constructor vs Assignment Demo
examples.description: Shows when copy constructor and assignment operator are called
examples.explanation: Box b = a uses copy constructor (new object). c = a uses assignment operator (existing object).

examples.title: Rule of Five Implementation
examples.description: Complete Rule of Five example with move semantics
examples.explanation: Rule of Five ensures proper resource management with both copy and move semantics in C++11.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for copy, O(1) for move
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for copy, O(1) for move

examples.title: Self-Assignment Check
examples.description: Importance of checking for self-assignment in assignment operator
examples.explanation: Self-assignment check prevents deleting source data before copying, which would cause undefined behavior.
